title: sun / sunpower / sunkissed
body:
# sun / sunpower / sunkissed

The "sun" register in Colin's alphabets-era output is wide enough to have
generated its own release group — **SUNPOWER**, **SUNPOWER2**, **SUNPOWER3**
— alongside scattered sun-titled tracks elsewhere in the archive. This
motif sits in his catalog the way "rain" sits in many singer-songwriter
catalogs: a touchstone he kept returning to.

## Releases that explicitly carry the motif as title

- **SUNPOWER** _(release in archive — _Documentation/Music/alphabets/SUNPOWER/_)_
- **SUNPOWER2** _(release — _Documentation/Music/alphabets/SUNPOWER2/_)_
- **SUNPOWER3** _(release — _Documentation/Music/alphabets/SUNPOWER3/_)_

Together these form an internal mini-series; see the `series/` folder if
that hasn't been formalized yet.

## Track-title appearances

- `SUNPOWER3/04 sunkissed goose.mp3`
- `SUNPOWER3/02 thunder bus.mp3` _(adjacent register — weather/charge)_
- `2011 [_]/sunkissed.mp3`
- `BESTUV09/14 sun mountain champions.m4a`
- `BESTUV09/02 sunlight monsters summer hello.m4a`
- `MAY2011DTE/...` _(May 2011 release — high overlap with sun-light register)_
- `09 [_]/sun likes stars field day 3 leg race.m4a` _(sun + stars overlap)_

## Reading

Where the **howl** motif sits in late-night/dream territory, the **sun**
motif is daytime, social, summer-coded — "summer hello", "field day", "sun
mountain champions". The SUNPOWER series specifically reads as Colin's
*celebratory* register: bright, playful, group-energy. Distinct from the
nocturnal "rave" or "ghost" registers elsewhere in his catalog.

## Notes for future passes

- "Sunkissed" appears multiple times — possibly a sub-motif worth its own
  trace.
- The relationship between SUNPOWER (the release group) and other "sunny"
  releases (e.g. CAVE RAVE's daytime moments, BIEBER JERBOA's "let go of
  stress") is worth mapping.
